title: Dative Prepositions
description: Learn words like 'with', 'from', and 'to'—and how they force the Dative case!
explanation:
**HOOK**
You just learned the 5 Akkusativ bosses. Now, meet the even bigger and bossier group: The Dativ Bosses! These words are super common—you can't say 'I sit WITH my friend' without them. They reach out and force every word after them to put on a Dativ nametag.

**CONCEPT**
We are learning 'Dativ-Prepositions'. These words: aus (from, out of), bei (at, by), mit (with), nach (after, to), and von (from) are special because they ALWAYS trigger the Dativ case.

**RULE**
Just remember: 'mit', 'aus', 'nach', 'von', 'bei', 'zu'... these are Dativ's best friends. If you see them, use 'dem', 'der', 'ihm', or 'mir' immediately after!

tables.title: The Big 6 (Dativ Bosses)
tables.note: TIP: These prepositions change der/das to dem, and die to der!

deep_notes.title: WATCH OUT
deep_notes.text: Remember: The articles in Dativ change spelling! Masculine/Neuter become '-m' (dem), and Feminine (die) jumps to 'der'. It's weird to say 'bei DER Mutter', but that's the Dativ boss at work!

exercises.question: Du gehst nicht alleine ins Kino. Du gehst ____ einem Freund.

exercises.text: Er (He) hat auch ein Ticket! Das heißt: Ich komme mit ____.

exercises.question: Du schreibst eine Karte zu einem Geschenk. Wer hat das Geschenk gegeben? Das Geschenk ist...
Wenn du über dein Heimatland sprichst: 'Ich komme ____ Deutschland.'
exercises.explanation: 'aus' wird für Länder und Städte (origin) genutzt!
